application.name: ModelShop eShop
application.description: A moderately advanced e-commerce application for modelling supplies, painting tools, RC cars and parts

SecurityConfig.java.summary: Configures Spring Security with JWT authentication, role-based access control, and stateless session management
SecurityConfig.java.description: SecurityConfig establishes the application security framework by defining public, authenticated, and admin-only endpoint access rules. It configures stateless JWT-based authentication using BCrypt password encoding and integrates the JWT filter into the Spring Security filter chain. The configuration implements role-based access control distinguishing ROLE_USER from ROLE_ADMIN permissions. All Swagger documentation endpoints are explicitly whitelisted for public access.
business_rules: Public endpoints: /auth/**, GET /categories/**, GET /products/**, /swagger-ui/**, /actuator/health
Admin-only endpoints: /admin/**
All other endpoints require authentication (any valid JWT)
BCrypt used for password hashing
Session management is STATELESS - no server-side sessions
JWT filter runs before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter

method_breakdown.summary: Creates BCryptPasswordEncoder bean for password hashing
method_breakdown.significance: Core security bean used by authentication manager and user registration

method_breakdown.summary: Defines URL-based security rules and filter chain
method_breakdown.significance: Central security configuration defining all endpoint access policies

CorsConfig.java.summary: Configures Cross-Origin Resource Sharing to allow frontend-to-backend communication
CorsConfig.java.description: CorsConfig creates a global CORS filter that permits requests from any origin, supports all HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, OPTIONS), and exposes the Authorization header. This configuration is intended for development and should be restricted in production environments.
business_rules: All origin patterns allowed (wildcard) - development configuration
HTTP methods allowed: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, OPTIONS
Authorization header exposed to clients
Credentials (cookies, auth headers) allowed

method_breakdown.summary: Creates and registers global CORS filter bean
method_breakdown.significance: Enables Angular frontend to communicate with Spring Boot backend across origins

SwaggerConfig.java.summary: Configures SpringFox Swagger 3.0 API documentation with JWT authentication support
SwaggerConfig.java.description: SwaggerConfig generates interactive OpenAPI documentation for all REST controllers. It configures JWT Bearer token authentication in the Swagger UI, allowing developers to test authenticated endpoints directly from the documentation interface.
business_rules: All controller endpoints in com.eshop.controller package are documented
JWT Bearer token authentication supported in Swagger UI
API title: ModelShop eShop API, version 1.0.0

method_breakdown.summary: Creates the main Swagger Docket bean with security configuration

AuthController.java.summary: Handles user authentication - login and registration REST endpoints
AuthController.java.description: AuthController exposes two public endpoints for user authentication. The login endpoint validates credentials and returns a JWT token. The registration endpoint creates a new user account and automatically logs them in by returning a JWT token. Both endpoints use input validation and return structured API responses.
business_rules: Login requires valid email format and non-blank password
Registration requires valid email, password (min 6 chars), first name, last name
Auto-login occurs after successful registration
JWT Bearer token returned in AuthResponse on success

method_breakdown.summary: Authenticates user with email/password and returns JWT token
method_breakdown.returns: ApiResponse<AuthResponse> containing JWT token and user info
method_breakdown.significance: Primary authentication entry point for the application

method_breakdown.summary: Creates a new user account and returns JWT token
parameters: request (RegisterRequest): email, password, firstName, lastName, optional phone
method_breakdown.returns: ApiResponse<AuthResponse> containing JWT token and user info
method_breakdown.significance: User onboarding endpoint - creates account and authenticates in one step

ProductController.java.summary: Provides REST endpoints for product catalog browsing and admin product management
ProductController.java.description: ProductController exposes public endpoints for browsing, searching, and filtering the product catalog, as well as admin-only endpoints for creating, updating, and deleting products. Products can be browsed by page, searched with text keywords, filtered by category/price/brand, and retrieved individually. Admin operations require ROLE_ADMIN authorization.
business_rules: GET endpoints are publicly accessible (no authentication required)
POST, PUT, DELETE require ROLE_ADMIN
Default page size is 12, featured products default to 8
Products can be filtered by keyword, category, price range, and brand
Products can be sorted by any field in ascending or descending order

method_breakdown.summary: Returns paginated list of all active products
Searches and filters products by multiple criteria
method_breakdown.significance: Core product discovery feature with multi-criteria filtering

method_breakdown.summary: Returns featured products for homepage display
Admin creates a new product
parameters: request (ProductRequest): full product details with validation

method_breakdown.summary: Admin updates an existing product
Admin hard-deletes a product

CartController.java.summary: Manages shopping cart operations - get, add, update, remove, clear for authenticated users
CartController.java.description: CartController provides a complete cart management API for authenticated users. Each user has exactly one persistent cart. Items can be added, updated, or removed. The cart is automatically created on first access and persists between sessions.
business_rules: All cart endpoints require authentication (JWT)
Each user has one cart (created on first access)
Cart operations use productId as the item identifier
Cart quantity must be at least 1

method_breakdown.summary: Returns the authenticated user's cart with all items and totals
method_breakdown.significance: Cart display endpoint used on cart page

method_breakdown.summary: Adds a product to cart or increments existing item quantity
Updates quantity of a specific cart item
Removes a specific item from the cart
Removes all items from the cart

OrderController.java.summary: Handles order placement and retrieval for users and admins
OrderController.java.description: OrderController manages the order lifecycle from placement to status updates. Users can place orders from their cart, view their order history, and check specific order details. Admins can view all orders and update order statuses. Access control ensures users can only view their own orders unless they have admin privileges.
business_rules: Orders are created from the user's current cart contents
Users can only view their own orders (admin can view any order)
Admin endpoint /admin/orders requires ROLE_ADMIN
Order status updates are admin-only
Order retrieval verifies ownership for non-admin users

method_breakdown.summary: Places a new order from the authenticated user's cart
parameters: request (OrderRequest): payment method, shipping address, notes

method_breakdown.summary: Returns paginated order history for the current user
Admin endpoint returning all orders across all users
Admin updates the fulfillment status of an order

CategoryController.java.summary: Provides category tree retrieval and admin category management endpoints
CategoryController.java.description: CategoryController exposes public endpoints to retrieve the hierarchical category tree and admin endpoints to manage categories. The root categories endpoint returns a nested tree structure including children for navigation menus. Category management (CRUD) is restricted to admins.
business_rules: GET endpoints are publicly accessible
POST, PUT, DELETE require ROLE_ADMIN
Root categories include their active children
Category slugs must be unique

method_breakdown.summary: Returns root-level categories with their child categories (cached)
method_breakdown.significance: Drives navigation menu with hierarchical category tree

method_breakdown.summary: Admin creates a new category with optional parent
parameters: request (CategoryRequest): name, description, slug, imageUrl, parentId, active

ReviewController.java.summary: Manages product reviews - get, create, and delete operations
ReviewController.java.description: ReviewController handles the product review system. Public users can view approved reviews for any product. Authenticated users can submit one review per product. Review deletion is allowed for the review owner or any admin. Reviews are automatically associated with the product's average rating calculation.
business_rules: GET product reviews is publicly accessible
POST review requires authentication
Each user can only review a product once (unique constraint)
Review deletion allowed only for review owner or admin
Only approved reviews are returned in listing
Rating must be between 1 and 5

method_breakdown.summary: Returns paginated approved reviews for a product
Authenticated user submits a review for a product
method_breakdown.significance: Customer feedback collection - triggers rating recalculation

method_breakdown.summary: Deletes a review (owner or admin only)

UserController.java.summary: User profile management and admin user listing endpoints
UserController.java.description: UserController provides endpoints for users to view and update their own profiles and for admins to list all users. The current user profile endpoint uses the JWT token to identify the user, ensuring users can only modify their own data.
business_rules: Users can view and update only their own profile
Profile update supports partial updates (null fields are ignored)
Admin can list all users - requires ROLE_ADMIN
Password is not updateable through profile endpoint

method_breakdown.summary: Returns the authenticated user's profile
Updates authenticated user's profile information

AuthService.java.summary: Handles user authentication - credential validation, registration, and JWT token generation
AuthService.java.description: AuthService orchestrates the authentication workflow. Login validates credentials through Spring AuthenticationManager, generates a JWT token, and returns user information. Registration checks for duplicate emails, hashes the password with BCrypt, creates the user, and then auto-logs them in. Both operations return an AuthResponse with the JWT token and user details.
business_rules: Email must be unique across all users (checked before registration)
Password is BCrypt-encoded before storage
Auto-login happens immediately after registration
JWT token is generated using user ID as subject with email and role as claims
Duplicate email throws DuplicateResourceException (HTTP 409)

method_breakdown.summary: Authenticates user credentials and generates JWT token
method_breakdown.returns: AuthResponse with JWT token, tokenType, userId, email, firstName, lastName, role
method_breakdown.significance: Core authentication method - validates credentials and issues JWT

method_breakdown.summary: Creates new user account with BCrypt-hashed password and auto-logs in
parameters: request (RegisterRequest): email, password, firstName, lastName, phone
method_breakdown.significance: User onboarding - enforces unique email and password hashing before creating account

OrderService.java.summary: Core order placement service with pricing calculations, stock validation, and cart clearance
OrderService.java.description: OrderService implements the complete order creation workflow. When an order is placed, it validates cart is non-empty, checks stock availability for each item, calculates the effective price (sale price takes precedence), applies free shipping for orders over £100, calculates 20% VAT, decrements product stock, saves the order, and clears the cart. Order numbers follow the pattern ORD-{timestamp}-{random}. Admin functions include listing all orders and updating fulfillment status.
business_rules: Cart must not be empty to place an order
Each product must have sufficient stock before order is confirmed
Sale price takes precedence over regular price when calculating order line totals
Free shipping applied for orders with subtotal >= £100
Standard shipping cost is £9.99 for orders under £100
Tax rate is 20% (VAT) applied on subtotal only
Payment status is automatically set to PAID (simulated payment)
Product stock is decremented immediately upon order placement
Cart is cleared after order is successfully placed
Order number format: ORD-{yyyyMMddHHmmss}-{4-digit-random}
Non-admin users can only access their own orders
Order snapshot captures productName and productSku at purchase time

method_breakdown.summary: Places a complete order from cart with pricing, tax, shipping, and stock updates
method_breakdown.significance: Most critical business method - orchestrates entire checkout workflow

method_breakdown.summary: Returns paginated order history for a specific user
Returns a specific order with ownership validation
parameters: isAdmin (boolean): skip ownership check for admins
method_breakdown.significance: Order detail view with access control

method_breakdown.summary: Admin updates order fulfillment status
Generates unique order reference number

CartService.java.summary: Manages shopping cart operations with stock validation and lazy cart creation
CartService.java.description: CartService handles all cart lifecycle operations. A cart is lazily created when a user first accesses it. When adding items, it validates product availability and stock. If the product already exists in the cart, quantities are merged with a stock check on the combined total. Cart responses include real-time effective pricing (sale price priority) and available stock for UI display.
business_rules: Cart is auto-created on first access (lazy initialization)
Only active products can be added to cart
Cannot add more quantity than available stock
If product already in cart, new quantity is added to existing quantity
Combined quantity cannot exceed available stock
Effective price uses sale price if available, otherwise regular price
Line total = effective price * quantity
Cart response includes available stock for each item (for UI quantity controls)

method_breakdown.summary: Adds product to cart or merges quantity if already present
method_breakdown.significance: Core add-to-cart with stock validation and quantity merging

method_breakdown.summary: Updates quantity of existing cart item with stock check
Removes a product from the user's cart
Returns existing cart or creates a new empty cart for user

ProductService.java.summary: Product catalog management - browsing, search, filtering, and admin CRUD operations
ProductService.java.description: ProductService provides the complete product catalog functionality. Products can be retrieved by ID, SKU, category, featured status, or through multi-criteria search. Admin operations support full CRUD with image management. The search functionality supports text search across name, description, and brand fields, combined with price range and category filters. SKU uniqueness is enforced on creation and update.
business_rules: Only active products are returned in public listings
SKU must be unique across all products
Sale price must be at least £0.01 if provided
Stock quantity cannot be negative
Product images are ordered by sortOrder field
Primary image is flagged and used as the thumbnail image
Updating SKU to an existing SKU throws DuplicateResourceException
Featured flag controls homepage featured section visibility

method_breakdown.summary: Returns paginated active products with sorting
Multi-criteria product search with keyword, category, price, brand filters
method_breakdown.significance: Drives the search and filter UX

method_breakdown.summary: Admin creates product with images and validates SKU uniqueness
method_breakdown.significance: Admin product creation with image handling

method_breakdown.summary: Admin updates product fields and replaces image collection
method_breakdown.significance: Admin product management with full image replacement

ReviewService.java.summary: Product review management with automatic average rating recalculation
ReviewService.java.description: ReviewService manages the review lifecycle. When a review is submitted, it checks for duplicate reviews (one per user per product), saves the review, and immediately recalculates the product's average rating and review count. Deletion also triggers rating recalculation. Only approved reviews are shown publicly and counted in the average.
business_rules: Each user can review a product only once
Rating must be between 1 and 5 (inclusive)
Only approved reviews are returned in public listing
Only approved reviews are included in average rating calculation
Average rating is recalculated after every create/delete
Review deletion is restricted to the review owner or an admin
Review title max 100 characters, comment max 2000 characters

method_breakdown.summary: Creates a review and recalculates product average rating
method_breakdown.significance: Customer feedback with automatic rating aggregation

method_breakdown.summary: Deletes review with ownership check and recalculates rating
Recalculates and saves product average rating from all approved reviews
method_breakdown.significance: Keeps product rating metrics consistent after review changes

CategoryService.java.summary: Category hierarchy management with caching for the product taxonomy tree
CategoryService.java.description: CategoryService manages the two-level category hierarchy. Root categories with their active children are cached to improve performance since category trees change infrequently. Cache is evicted on any category create, update, or delete. Category slugs must be unique and are used for URL-friendly category identification. Only active categories and children are returned in public listings.
business_rules: Category names and slugs must be globally unique
Slug uniqueness is checked on create and update
Categories can have one optional parent (two-level tree: root -> children)
Only active categories are included in root category listing
Only active children are returned in category tree
Category tree (root list) is cached under key 'categories'
Cache is evicted on any create, update, or delete operation

method_breakdown.summary: Returns active root categories with their active children (cached)
method_breakdown.significance: Drives navigation menu - cached for performance

method_breakdown.summary: Creates new category with optional parent assignment
parameters: request (CategoryRequest): name, description, slug, imageUrl, parentId, active
method_breakdown.significance: Admin taxonomy management - evicts category cache

method_breakdown.summary: Updates category and optionally reparents it
parameters: request (CategoryRequest): updated fields including optional parentId
method_breakdown.significance: Admin taxonomy maintenance - evicts category cache

UserService.java.summary: User profile management - retrieval, update, and admin user listing
UserService.java.description: UserService provides profile management for users and admin operations. Profile updates apply only non-null fields, supporting partial updates. The service maps between User entities and UserResponse DTOs. Admins can access paginated lists of all users.
business_rules: Profile update is partial - only non-null fields are updated
Password is not exposed in UserResponse (excluded from mapping)
Users are identified by their database ID (from JWT token)
Address update replaces the entire embedded address object

method_breakdown.summary: Partially updates user profile - only updates non-null fields
parameters: request (UpdateProfileRequest): nullable firstName, lastName, phone, address
method_breakdown.significance: User self-service profile management with partial update support

JwtTokenProvider.java.summary: JWT token generation and validation using HMAC-SHA512 signature
JwtTokenProvider.java.description: JwtTokenProvider generates stateless JWT tokens containing user ID as subject, email and role as claims. Tokens are signed with HMAC-SHA512 using a configurable secret key. Token expiration is configurable (default 24 hours = 86400000ms). Validation catches and logs all JWT exception types including expired, malformed, and unsupported tokens.
business_rules: JWT token contains: subject=userId, email claim, role claim
Token signed with HS512 algorithm
Default expiration: 24 hours (86400000ms)
Refresh token expiration: 7 days (604800000ms)
Invalid/expired tokens return false on validation (do not throw)

method_breakdown.summary: Creates signed JWT with user ID, email, role and expiry
method_breakdown.significance: Generates authentication token returned to client

method_breakdown.summary: Extracts user ID from JWT claims subject
Validates JWT signature, expiry and format
method_breakdown.significance: Security gate - prevents invalid/expired token usage

JwtAuthenticationFilter.java.summary: Servlet filter that validates JWT tokens and sets Spring Security authentication context
JwtAuthenticationFilter.java.description: JwtAuthenticationFilter intercepts every HTTP request, extracts the Bearer token from the Authorization header, validates it, loads the corresponding user, and sets the authentication in the Spring Security context. It executes once per request and continues the filter chain regardless of authentication result.
business_rules: Token extracted from Authorization header with 'Bearer ' prefix
Invalid or missing tokens result in unauthenticated request (not rejected - SecurityConfig handles that)
Authentication is loaded from database on every request (no token caching)

method_breakdown.summary: Extracts and validates JWT, sets Spring Security authentication context
method_breakdown.significance: Core request authentication filter - runs on every HTTP request

method_breakdown.summary: Extracts Bearer token from Authorization header
method_breakdown.significance: HTTP header parsing for Bearer token extraction

UserDetailsServiceImpl.java.summary: Spring Security UserDetailsService implementation loading users by email or ID
UserDetailsServiceImpl.java.description: UserDetailsServiceImpl bridges Spring Security authentication with the application's user repository. It loads users by email (for credential-based login) and by ID (for JWT token-based authentication). Creates UserPrincipal instances wrapping the User entity with Spring Security's UserDetails interface.
business_rules: Users are looked up by email for username/password authentication
Users are looked up by ID for JWT-based request authentication
UsernameNotFoundException thrown if user not found (standard Spring Security)

method_breakdown.summary: Loads UserDetails by email address for Spring Security login
method_breakdown.significance: Spring Security integration for credential authentication

method_breakdown.summary: Loads UserDetails by user ID for JWT filter authentication
method_breakdown.significance: JWT-to-user resolution on each authenticated request

UserPrincipal.java.summary: Spring Security UserDetails implementation wrapping the User entity
UserPrincipal.java.description: UserPrincipal adapts the application User entity to implement Spring Security's UserDetails interface. It holds the user's ID, email, hashed password, role, and derived authorities. The static factory method creates instances from User entities with the role mapped to a GrantedAuthority.
business_rules: Username is the user's email address
Single authority per user matching their UserRole enum name
Account is always non-expired, non-locked, and credentials non-expired (enabled check at User entity level)

method_breakdown.summary: Factory method creating UserPrincipal from User entity
method_breakdown.significance: Converts domain User to Spring Security principal

method_breakdown.summary: Returns email as the username for Spring Security

JwtAuthenticationEntryPoint.java.summary: Returns 401 Unauthorized JSON response for unauthenticated requests to protected endpoints
JwtAuthenticationEntryPoint.java.description: JwtAuthenticationEntryPoint is invoked when an unauthenticated request attempts to access a protected resource. Instead of redirecting to a login page (typical for web apps), it returns a JSON error response with HTTP 401 status, appropriate for REST API consumers.
business_rules: Unauthenticated requests to protected endpoints receive HTTP 401
Response body is JSON ApiResponse with success=false and error message

method_breakdown.summary: Writes 401 JSON response for unauthorized access attempts

GlobalExceptionHandler.java.summary: Centralized exception handler mapping domain exceptions to HTTP responses
GlobalExceptionHandler.java.description: GlobalExceptionHandler uses @RestControllerAdvice to intercept all exceptions thrown from controllers and map them to appropriate HTTP status codes and JSON error responses. It handles resource not found (404), bad requests (400), duplicate resources (409), authentication failures (401), access denied (403), and validation errors (400 with field-level details).
business_rules: ResourceNotFoundException maps to HTTP 404
BadRequestException maps to HTTP 400
DuplicateResourceException maps to HTTP 409 Conflict
BadCredentialsException maps to HTTP 401 with generic message
AccessDeniedException maps to HTTP 403
MethodArgumentNotValidException maps to HTTP 400 with field error map
Unhandled exceptions map to HTTP 500

method_breakdown.summary: Maps bean validation failures to field-level error map
method_breakdown.significance: User-friendly validation error responses with per-field messages

User.java.summary: JPA entity representing a registered user with embedded address and role
User.java.description: User is the central identity entity. Each user has a unique email, BCrypt-hashed password, personal details, optional phone, role (USER or ADMIN), embedded delivery address, and enabled status. Users are the owner of carts, orders, and reviews.
business_rules: Email must be unique
Default role is ROLE_USER
Default enabled is true
Password stored as BCrypt hash

Product.java.summary: JPA entity representing a product in the catalog with pricing, stock, and images
Product.java.description: Product holds all catalog information including regular price, optional sale price, stock quantity, brand, scale (for models), specifications, images, featured flag, and active status. Products belong to a category. Average rating and review count are denormalized for performance.
business_rules: SKU must be unique
Default stock quantity is 0
Default active is true
Default featured is false
Average rating initialized to 0.00
Sale price is optional - when present it takes precedence in pricing

Order.java.summary: JPA entity representing a placed order with pricing breakdown and shipping details
Order.java.description: Order captures the complete purchase including order items, financial breakdown (subtotal, shipping, tax, total), shipping address snapshot, payment method, payment status, and order status. The order number is a unique human-readable reference.
business_rules: Order number must be unique
Default status is PENDING
Default payment status is PENDING
Default shipping cost is 0
Default tax is 0

Cart.java.summary: JPA entity for a user's persistent shopping cart (one per user)
Cart.java.description: Cart has a one-to-one relationship with User and holds a collection of CartItems. The cart persists between sessions and is created lazily. Cascade delete removes cart when user is deleted.
business_rules: One cart per user (unique constraint on user_id)
Cart items cascade on cart deletion
Cart deletion cascades when user is deleted

CartItem.java.summary: JPA entity for a product-quantity pair within a shopping cart
CartItem.java.description: CartItem links a Cart to a Product with a quantity. The quantity is persisted and validated to be > 0 at the database level. Each product can appear only once per cart (unique constraint at DB level).
business_rules: Quantity must be greater than 0 (DB CHECK constraint)
Each product unique per cart (UNIQUE(cart_id, product_id))

OrderItem.java.summary: JPA entity capturing product purchase snapshot within an order
OrderItem.java.description: OrderItem stores a snapshot of product name and SKU at purchase time, ensuring historical accuracy even if the product is later modified or deleted. Product reference is nullable (SET NULL on delete) while productName/productSku remain as immutable snapshots.
business_rules: productName and productSku are snapshots captured at order time
Product reference can become null if product is deleted (SET NULL)
Unit price and total price are captured at order time
Quantity must be > 0 (DB CHECK constraint)

Review.java.summary: JPA entity for a product review with rating, title, and comment
Review.java.description: Review associates a user with a product along with a 1-5 star rating, optional title, and optional comment. Each user-product pair has a unique constraint preventing duplicate reviews. Reviews have an approved flag for moderation.
business_rules: Rating must be between 1 and 5 (DB CHECK constraint)
Each user can review a product only once (UNIQUE(user_id, product_id))
Default approved is true
Reviews are soft-filtered by approved flag in queries

Category.java.summary: JPA entity for hierarchical product categories with slug-based URLs
Category.java.description: Category supports a two-level hierarchy with an optional parent reference and a children collection. Name and slug are unique. Slug is used for URL-friendly category identification. Active flag controls visibility.
business_rules: Name and slug must be globally unique
Default active is true
Parent deletion sets parent_id to NULL (not cascade delete children)
Children collection is cascade-all with orphan removal

ProductImage.java.summary: JPA entity for product images with primary flag and display ordering
ProductImage.java.description: ProductImage stores image URLs associated with products. One image per product can be marked as primary for use as thumbnail. Images are ordered by sortOrder for consistent gallery display.
business_rules: One image per product can be marked as primary
Images are displayed in sortOrder sequence
Image cascade deletes with product (ON DELETE CASCADE)

ProductRepository.java.summary: Spring Data JPA repository with custom JPQL queries for product search and filtering
ProductRepository.java.description: ProductRepository extends JpaRepository with custom finder methods for active products by category, featured products, and complex multi-criteria search. The findWithFilters query supports optional parameters (NULL is treated as 'no filter') for flexible catalog browsing.
business_rules: findByActiveTrue - only non-archived products in catalog
findByFeaturedTrueAndActiveTrue - featured section filter
searchByKeyword - case-insensitive LIKE search across name, description, brand
findWithFilters - all-optional parameter query for category, price, brand, keyword

OrderRepository.java.summary: Spring Data JPA repository for orders with user and status-based queries
OrderRepository.java.description: OrderRepository provides paginated order retrieval by user ID and order status lookup. The findByOrderNumber method enables order reference lookups.
business_rules: Orders are retrieved by userId with pagination and sorting
Order status filtering supported
Unique order number lookup supported

ReviewRepository.java.summary: Spring Data JPA repository for reviews with approved filter and duplicate detection
ReviewRepository.java.description: ReviewRepository queries reviews by product ID filtered to approved-only status. The existsByUserIdAndProductId method provides duplicate review detection before creating a new review.
business_rules: Only approved reviews returned in product review listings
Duplicate detection via existsByUserIdAndProductId

CategoryRepository.java.summary: Spring Data JPA repository for category hierarchy and slug-based lookups
CategoryRepository.java.description: CategoryRepository supports hierarchical category queries (root categories with null parent, children by parent ID), active category filtering, and slug uniqueness checking.
business_rules: Root categories have null parent
existsBySlug enables slug uniqueness validation
findByParentIsNull returns root-level categories

RegisterRequest.java.summary: Request DTO for user registration with bean validation
RegisterRequest.java.description: RegisterRequest carries user registration data with comprehensive validation: email format, password length (6-100 chars), name length (max 50 chars), and phone length (max 20 chars).
business_rules: Email must be valid format and non-blank
Password: 6-100 characters
First name: non-blank, max 50 characters
Last name: non-blank, max 50 characters
Phone: optional, max 20 characters

LoginRequest.java.summary: Request DTO for user login with email and password validation
LoginRequest.java.description: LoginRequest carries login credentials with validation for email format and password length requirements.
business_rules: Email must be valid format and non-blank
Password: 6-100 characters, non-blank

ProductRequest.java.summary: Request DTO for product creation/update with comprehensive validation
ProductRequest.java.description: ProductRequest carries all product data with validation rules. Price must be at least £0.01. Stock quantity cannot be negative. Category ID is required. Name max 200 chars, SKU max 50, brand max 100, scale max 50.
business_rules: Name: non-blank, max 200 characters
SKU: non-blank, max 50 characters
Price: required, minimum £0.01
Sale price: optional, minimum £0.01 if provided
Stock quantity: required, minimum 0
Category ID: required
Brand: optional, max 100 characters
Scale: optional, max 50 characters
Active defaults to true

OrderRequest.java.summary: Request DTO for order placement requiring payment method and shipping address
OrderRequest.java.description: OrderRequest carries checkout data. Payment method is required. Shipping address is required and validated (nested @Valid). Notes are optional.
business_rules: Payment method: required, non-blank
Shipping address: required with nested validation
Notes: optional

ReviewRequest.java.summary: Request DTO for review submission with 1-5 star rating validation
ReviewRequest.java.description: ReviewRequest carries review data with strict rating bounds (1-5 inclusive) and length limits on title and comment.
business_rules: Rating: required, 1-5 inclusive
Title: optional, max 100 characters
Comment: optional, max 2000 characters

CartItemRequest.java.summary: Request DTO for cart item add/update with positive quantity constraint
CartItemRequest.java.description: CartItemRequest carries the product ID and quantity for cart operations. Quantity must be at least 1.
business_rules: Product ID: required
Quantity: required, minimum 1

app.module.ts.summary: Root Angular module bootstrapping the application with HTTP interceptors
app.module.ts.description: AppModule is the root Angular module that imports shared components, sets up HTTP client, routing, and registers the two HTTP interceptors (auth and error handling). AuthInterceptor adds JWT tokens to requests and ErrorInterceptor handles 401 responses.
business_rules: AuthInterceptor runs on all HTTP requests to inject JWT Bearer token
ErrorInterceptor handles 401 responses by auto-logging out the user

app-routing.module.ts.summary: Defines lazy-loaded Angular routes with authentication and admin guards
app-routing.module.ts.description: App routing defines seven feature modules with lazy loading. Cart, checkout, and account routes are protected by AuthGuard (requires login). Admin routes are protected by AdminGuard (requires ROLE_ADMIN). Unknown routes redirect to home.
business_rules: Cart route requires authentication (AuthGuard)
Checkout route requires authentication (AuthGuard)
Account route requires authentication (AuthGuard)
Admin route requires admin role (AdminGuard)
Unknown paths redirect to home route

auth.service.ts.summary: Client-side authentication service managing JWT token storage and user state
auth.service.ts.description: AuthService manages the complete frontend authentication lifecycle. It maintains a reactive BehaviorSubject of the current user, persists the JWT token and user data in localStorage, and provides computed properties for login state and admin role. Login/register calls store auth data and emit to subscribers. Logout clears storage and redirects to login.
business_rules: JWT token stored in localStorage with key 'eshop_token'
User data stored in localStorage with key 'eshop_user'
Auth state is reactive via BehaviorSubject (currentUser$)
isAdmin checks role === 'ROLE_ADMIN'
Logout clears localStorage and navigates to /auth/login
Auth data persisted across browser sessions via localStorage

method_breakdown.summary: Posts login request and stores auth response in localStorage
Posts registration request and stores auth response in localStorage
Clears localStorage auth data and redirects to login
Computed property checking if user is authenticated
Computed property checking if user has ROLE_ADMIN

cart.service.ts.summary: Client-side cart service managing cart state with reactive BehaviorSubject
cart.service.ts.description: CartService maintains a reactive cart state using BehaviorSubject, enabling real-time cart count badge in the header. All cart mutations (add, update, remove, clear) update the local state after successful API calls. The cartItemCount property provides the total quantity for the header badge.
business_rules: Cart state is reactive via BehaviorSubject (cart$)
Cart count reflects total items across all cart entries
clearLocalCart resets local state without API call (used on logout)
Cart state automatically updates header badge via cart$ observable

method_breakdown.summary: Adds product to cart and updates reactive state
Updates cart item quantity
Computed property returning total items in cart

product.service.ts.summary: Client-side product service for catalog browsing, search, and review operations
product.service.ts.description: ProductService provides all product-related API calls including paginated browsing, multi-criteria search, category filtering, individual product retrieval, and review management. Admin operations (create, update, delete) are also included.
business_rules: Search accepts optional keyword, categoryId, minPrice, maxPrice, brand filters
Featured products default to page size 8
Reviews are paginated with default size 10

method_breakdown.summary: Searches products with optional multi-criteria filters
parameters: filter (ProductFilter): optional keyword, categoryId, minPrice, maxPrice, brand, page, size
method_breakdown.significance: Drives product list search and filter UI

method_breakdown.summary: Submits a product review

order.service.ts.summary: Client-side order service for order placement and history
order.service.ts.description: OrderService wraps all order-related API calls. createOrder submits the checkout form data. getMyOrders retrieves paginated order history. Admin operations include getAllOrders and updateOrderStatus.
business_rules: Order creation requires paymentMethod, shippingAddress, optional notes
Admin order status update uses query parameter not request body

method_breakdown.summary: Places a new order from checkout form data
Admin updates order fulfillment status

category.service.ts.summary: Client-side category service for navigation tree and admin category management
category.service.ts.description: CategoryService retrieves the category hierarchy for navigation menus and provides admin CRUD operations. getRootCategories returns the full tree for the navigation dropdown.
business_rules: Root categories drive the navigation dropdown menu
All categories endpoint returns flat list for admin category management

user.service.ts.summary: Client-side user service for profile management and admin user listing
user.service.ts.description: UserService handles profile retrieval (getMe), profile updates, and admin user listing. Profile updates are sent as partial objects to the backend.
business_rules: getMe uses JWT token in Authorization header (added by interceptor)
Profile update supports partial updates

auth.guard.ts.summary: Route guard redirecting unauthenticated users to login
auth.guard.ts.description: AuthGuard protects authenticated routes. If the user is not logged in, they are redirected to /auth/login and route activation is denied.
business_rules: Unauthenticated users redirected to /auth/login
isLoggedIn check based on localStorage JWT token

method_breakdown.summary: Checks authentication status and redirects if not logged in
method_breakdown.significance: Protects cart, checkout, and account routes

admin.guard.ts.summary: Route guard allowing only users with ROLE_ADMIN to access admin routes
admin.guard.ts.description: AdminGuard protects admin routes by checking both login status and admin role. Non-admin users are redirected to the home page.
business_rules: Must be logged in AND have ROLE_ADMIN to access admin routes
Non-admin authenticated users are redirected to home (not login)

method_breakdown.summary: Checks admin role and redirects non-admins to home
method_breakdown.significance: Protects all admin feature module routes

auth.interceptor.ts.summary: HTTP interceptor adding JWT Bearer token to all outgoing requests
auth.interceptor.ts.description: AuthInterceptor runs on every HTTP request and, if a JWT token is available in localStorage, clones the request and adds the Authorization: Bearer <token> header. Requests without a stored token are passed through unchanged.
business_rules: JWT token added to all requests if available in localStorage
Authorization header format: 'Bearer <token>'
Requests without token are passed through unchanged

method_breakdown.summary: Adds Authorization header to authenticated requests

error.interceptor.ts.summary: HTTP interceptor auto-logging out users on 401 Unauthorized responses
error.interceptor.ts.description: ErrorInterceptor catches HTTP errors from all API responses. When a 401 Unauthorized is received, it automatically calls logout to clear the invalid session and redirect to login. Other errors are re-thrown for component-level handling.
business_rules: HTTP 401 responses trigger automatic logout
Other HTTP errors are propagated to calling components
Expired or invalid JWT tokens cause automatic session termination

method_breakdown.summary: Catches HTTP errors and handles 401 with auto-logout
method_breakdown.significance: Automatic session cleanup on expired/invalid JWT

login.component.ts.summary: Login form component with email/password validation and error feedback
login.component.ts.description: LoginComponent presents the login form using Angular Reactive Forms with email format and minimum password length validation. On success, it navigates to the home page. On failure, it displays error messages via Angular Material Snackbar.
business_rules: Email format validation enforced client-side
Password minimum 6 characters enforced client-side
Failed login shows server error message or generic fallback
Successful login redirects to home page

register.component.ts.summary: Registration form with personal details, email, and password validation
register.component.ts.description: RegisterComponent presents the registration form requiring first name, last name, email, and password. Optional phone field is included. On success, navigates to home with welcome message.
business_rules: First name: required, max 50 chars
Last name: required, max 50 chars
Email: required, valid format
Password: required, min 6 chars
Phone: optional
Success redirects to home with 'Welcome to ModelShop!' message

home.component.ts.summary: Homepage displaying featured products and root category navigation
home.component.ts.description: HomeComponent loads 8 featured products and root categories on initialization. It serves as the entry point for product discovery, displaying the category navigation grid and featured product carousel/grid.
business_rules: Homepage displays up to 8 featured products
All root categories shown for navigation

product-list.component.ts.summary: Product listing with real-time multi-criteria filtering, sorting, and pagination
product-list.component.ts.description: ProductListComponent provides the main product catalog browsing experience. It combines reactive form filters (keyword, category, price range, brand, sort) with debounced automatic updates. URL query params (keyword, categoryId) are read on init to support navigation from search and category clicks. Filter changes reset to page 0.
business_rules: Search/filter debounced 400ms to prevent excessive API calls
Filter change resets to page 0
URL query params (keyword, categoryId) pre-populate filter form
Default sort: createdAt descending
Default page size: 12

method_breakdown.summary: Loads products from API using current filter form values
Resets all filters to default values

product-detail.component.ts.summary: Product detail page with image gallery, add-to-cart, and review submission
product-detail.component.ts.description: ProductDetailComponent displays full product information including image gallery (primary image selected by default), specifications, pricing (effective price using sale price if available), stock-aware quantity selector, add-to-cart action, and review list with review submission form. Redirects to products if product not found.
business_rules: Unauthenticated users attempting add-to-cart are redirected to login
Quantity selector bounded by stock quantity
Add-to-cart is disabled while request is in flight
Review form: rating 1-5 required, comment max 2000 chars
Product not found redirects to /products
Effective price: salePrice ?? price
Discount percent calculated: round((price - salePrice) / price * 100)

method_breakdown.summary: Adds product to cart with auth check and quantity
Submits product review and prepends to review list
Returns sale price if available, otherwise regular price

cart.component.ts.summary: Shopping cart page with item management and order total calculation
cart.component.ts.description: CartComponent displays the user's cart items with quantity controls, removal options, and real-time order total calculation. The shipping (free over £100) and 20% VAT calculations are mirrored from the backend, providing a consistent pre-checkout summary.
business_rules: Quantity must be >= 1 (prevents decrement below 1)
Free shipping for subtotal >= £100, otherwise £9.99
Tax is 20% of subtotal
Total = subtotal + shipping + tax
Cart is loaded on component init and subscribes to reactive cart state

method_breakdown.summary: Calculates shipping cost: £9.99 if under £100, £0 if over
Calculates 20% VAT on subtotal
Calculates order total including shipping and tax

checkout.component.ts.summary: Multi-step checkout with shipping form, payment selection, and order placement
checkout.component.ts.description: CheckoutComponent presents the checkout form in two reactive form groups: shipping (address) and payment (method selection and notes). It pre-populates the shipping form with the user's saved delivery address. Payment methods include Credit/Debit Card, PayPal, and Bank Transfer. On order placement, it calls the OrderService, clears the local cart state, and displays the order confirmation.
business_rules: Shipping address fields: street (required), city (required), state (optional), zipCode (required), country (required)
Payment method required (defaults to CREDIT_CARD)
User's saved address pre-populates shipping form
Available payment methods: CREDIT_CARD, PAYPAL, BANK_TRANSFER
Order placed if both forms are valid
Cart cleared locally on successful order placement
Free shipping >= £100, otherwise £9.99
Tax is 20% of subtotal

method_breakdown.summary: Validates forms and submits order to backend
method_breakdown.significance: Final checkout action - creates order and shows confirmation

method_breakdown.summary: Calculates shipping cost based on cart subtotal
Calculates 20% tax on subtotal

profile.component.ts.summary: User profile editing form for personal details and delivery address
profile.component.ts.description: ProfileComponent loads the current user's profile and populates a reactive form with personal details (firstName, lastName, phone) and a nested address group. On save, the updated profile is sent to the backend and the local state is updated.
business_rules: Profile loads on init from GET /users/me
All fields are optional for update (partial update supported)
Address is nested form group with all fields optional

orders.component.ts.summary: Order history listing with status color coding
orders.component.ts.description: OrdersComponent displays the authenticated user's paginated order history. Each order status has a CSS class for color coding (pending=yellow, shipped=blue, delivered=green, cancelled=red).
business_rules: Orders listed in reverse chronological order
Status visual coding: PENDING=yellow, CONFIRMED=dark, PROCESSING=orange, SHIPPED=blue, DELIVERED=green, CANCELLED=red

dashboard.component.ts.summary: Admin dashboard with aggregate stats for products, orders, and users
dashboard.component.ts.description: DashboardComponent loads summary statistics in parallel using forkJoin: total product count, total order count, total user count, and the 5 most recent orders. This provides a quick operational overview.
business_rules: Dashboard shows totalProducts, totalOrders, totalUsers, and 5 most recent orders
Data loaded in parallel with forkJoin for performance

admin-orders.component.ts.summary: Admin order management with paginated listing and inline status updates
admin-orders.component.ts.description: AdminOrdersComponent provides admins with a paginated table of all orders with columns for order number, user, status, total, date, and actions. Admins can update order status directly from the table using a dropdown. Available statuses: PENDING, CONFIRMED, PROCESSING, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED.
business_rules: Admin can update order status to: PENDING, CONFIRMED, PROCESSING, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED
Table columns: orderNumber, user, status, total, date, actions
Status updates are immediately reflected in the table

admin-products.component.ts.summary: Admin product management table with delete confirmation
admin-products.component.ts.description: AdminProductsComponent provides admins with a paginated table of all products. Admins can delete products with a browser confirm dialog. Table shows ID, name, SKU, price, stock, category, featured, and active status.
business_rules: Delete requires browser confirm dialog confirmation
Table columns: id, name, sku, price, stock, category, featured, active, actions
Products reloaded after deletion

header.component.ts.summary: Global navigation header with search, cart badge, user menu, and category dropdown
header.component.ts.description: HeaderComponent provides the global navigation bar visible on all pages. It loads root categories for the dropdown menu, subscribes to auth state to show/hide authenticated menus, subscribes to cart state for real-time item count badge, and handles the search functionality by navigating to the product list with the keyword query param.
business_rules: Cart is loaded when user logs in (reactive auth subscription)
Search navigates to /products with keyword query param
Cart badge shows total item count from reactive cart state
User menu shown when logged in, login/register links when not
Logout clears local cart state before calling auth logout
Category dropdown shows root categories

method_breakdown.summary: Navigates to product list with keyword query parameter
Clears cart state and calls auth service logout

product-card.component.ts.summary: Reusable product card with sale price display, discount badge, and quick add-to-cart
product-card.component.ts.description: ProductCardComponent is the reusable product display card used in product lists and home page. It shows product image, name, pricing (with sale price and discount percentage), stock status, and an add-to-cart button. Unauthenticated users clicking add-to-cart are redirected to login.
business_rules: Add to cart redirects to login if not authenticated
Effective price: salePrice if present, otherwise price
Discount percent: round((price - salePrice) / price * 100)
isInStock: stockQuantity > 0
Stock status displayed on card
Discount badge displayed when salePrice is present

method_breakdown.summary: Returns salePrice if available, otherwise regular price
Calculates percentage discount for discount badge
Returns true if stockQuantity > 0

V1__init_schema.sql.summary: Initial database schema creating all tables, constraints, and performance indexes
V1__init_schema.sql.description: The initial Flyway migration creates the complete relational schema: users, categories (with self-referential parent), products, product_images, carts, cart_items (unique per cart-product), orders, order_items (with product snapshot columns), and reviews (unique per user-product). CHECK constraints enforce business rules at the database level. Indexes optimize common query patterns.
business_rules: cart_items: quantity CHECK > 0, UNIQUE(cart_id, product_id)
order_items: quantity CHECK > 0, product_id SET NULL on product delete
reviews: rating CHECK 1-5, UNIQUE(user_id, product_id)
categories: parent_id SET NULL on parent delete
products: category_id NOT NULL (FK to categories)
Indexes: products.category_id, products.active, products.featured, order_items.order_id, orders.user_id, reviews.product_id, cart_items.cart_id

V2__seed_data.sql.summary: Seeds initial product catalog with categories, products, and default user accounts
V2__seed_data.sql.description: The seed data migration creates the initial product taxonomy (4 root categories with 11 subcategories), 18 sample products across all categories, and 2 user accounts (admin and test user). All passwords use a shared BCrypt hash. Product data represents real hobby brands (Tamiya, Vallejo, Traxxas, Iwata, etc.) with accurate pricing.
business_rules: Admin account: admin@modelshop.com with ROLE_ADMIN
Test user account: john@example.com with ROLE_USER
Root categories: Scale Models, Paints & Painting Tools, RC Cars & Parts, Tools & Accessories
18 sample products across 11 subcategories
Featured products: Tiger I, M1A2 Abrams, Spitfire Mk.IXc, Vallejo WWII Set, Iwata Eclipse, Tamiya TT-02, Traxxas Rustler
All passwords use same BCrypt hash in seed (development only)
